model_phrasing: Model phrasing: GPT-5.x detected from the system harness; Phrasing profile id RTM-PH6OCQ6MX4.
decision_target: Choose the exact session deactivation cleanup contract without changing current-owned activation or adding a Lite primitive.
source_of_truth: workers/dkr-1-cleanup/cleanup-contract.json, its saved executable probe, current Lite and SDK source, and the independent 6-of-6 activation validator record
read_method: Run workers/dkr-1-cleanup/replay.sh and compare the asserted case counts, commit counts, mutation counts, race outcomes, cleanup order, and source hashes with this checkpoint.
questions_answered: Ordinary deactivation must synchronously fence the activation, abort all live attempts, and join all snapshotted settlements without beginning durable finish or calling store.commit.
Cancellation may settle activation-local work and attempt records as cancelled. Those working-copy changes are disposable until an explicit checkpoint or finish commits a snapshot.
Ordinary deactivation leaves both the activation-local SessionRecord status/version and the durable store status/version unchanged.
The smallest placement is SessionRuntime.deactivate, replacing shutdown, invoked by the existing resource cleanup closure. Lite already supplies the needed cleanup timing and error policy.
Repeated deactivation returns one cached promise and repeats no effects.
Finish and deactivation use first-claimer ordering: finish-first commits once and deactivation awaits it; deactivate-first rejects later finish and commits zero times.
An explicit checkpoint before close remains the only checkpoint commit; an explicit finish before close remains the only finished commit.
Settlement rejection waits for all snapshotted attempts, then rejects cleanup without a commit. Ordinary cancelled settlement is a value, not an error.
Existing Lite order is execution onClose first, then resources in reverse resolution order, with each cleanup awaited.
The prior current-owned activation behavior remains independently verified at 6 of 6.
questions_unanswered: Independent validation has not yet replayed the ten cleanup cases or challenged the proposed public method shape.
Parent-child structured cancellation and provider-specific descendant joining remain assigned to DKR-2, not this cleanup checkpoint.
The product still has shutdown calling beginFinish, so the cleanup wall remains breached until a separately authorized progression change is implemented and verified.
decision: candidate only; never accepted by this worker

wall_gate.reason: Current product cleanup still changes the runtime SessionRecord status to finishing, and the new cleanup contract lacks independent replay.

worked_checkpoint_trace.decision: rejected for downstream advance because the current product wall is breached

confidence_probability_update: 0.55 before the cleanup cases; 0.92 after ten candidate cases, current baseline replay, Lite order replay, and preserved 6-of-6 activation replay
risk_or_anti_goal_implications: The contract removes the hidden beginFinish effect from ordinary resource cleanup without weakening cancellation or join.
Separating activation lifecycle from SessionRecord status is required to fence late work while leaving durable business state unchanged.
First-claimer finish ordering prevents a deactivation race from creating an unrequested commit.
Current product state still breaches cleanup_business_state_mutation_count, so this learning cannot authorize progression by itself.
No Lite change is supported by the probe.

candidate_ckrs.target: 10/10 cleanup cases while cleanup_business_state_mutation_count remains 0 after implementation

candidate_pkrs.scope: Replace shutdown with deactivation, add activation lifecycle arbitration, and enforce the ten scope-seam cases after checkpoint acceptance.

checkpoint_rejection_policy: Superficial, tautological, false-positive, fabricated, unsupported, or single-LLM-truth checkpoints are rejected. Missing, stale, wrong-source, non-replayable, or contradicted evidence fails closed. An empty result remains valid only with a replayable probe trace.
role_grammar: CKRs are measurable contribution context, not worker work.
DKRs are discovery-worker scopes; PKRs are progression-worker execution units; there is no CKR worker.
